title: Add worktree serialization short-circuit to _beads_conflict and _find_conflict_reason
description: In `src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py`, add a short-circuit at the top of the same-feature-tree branch of `_beads_conflict()` (around line 421): when `config.scheduler.serialize_within_feature_tree` is `True`, both beads are mutating agents (`developer`, `tester`, `documentation`), and they share a feature tree, return `True` immediately. Also add a matching early-return in `_find_conflict_reason()` (around line 357) that returns `"worktree serialization enabled — waiting on in-progress {active.bead_id}"` before existing checks when the same three conditions hold. Use the existing `MUTATING_AGENTS` constant already in scope — no new imports needed.
acceptance_criteria: With flag `False`, `_beads_conflict()` behaviour is unchanged for all bead pairs
With flag `True`, two mutating beads in the same feature tree with non-overlapping `expected_files` return `True` from `_beads_conflict()`
With flag `True`, two mutating beads in different feature trees return `False` from `_beads_conflict()`
With flag `True`, a non-mutating bead (planner/review/recovery) paired with a mutating bead in the same tree returns `False` from `_beads_conflict()`
`_find_conflict_reason()` returns `"worktree serialization enabled — waiting on in-progress <bead_id>"` when the new rule fires
New reason string precedes existing checks in `_find_conflict_reason()`
